entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: Cicer arietinum L., the crop being studied for resistance to Ascochyta blight

entities.name: ASCOCHYTA BLIGHT
entities.description: A foliar disease caused by the pathogen Ascochyta rabiei

entities.name: GENETIC RESISTANCE
entities.description: The inherent ability of chickpea cultivars to resist infection by Ascochyta blight

entities.name: COOL AND HUMID WEATHER
entities.description: Environmental conditions that promote the development and spread of Ascochyta blight

entities.name: INOCULUM AVAILABILITY
entities.description: The presence and quantity of the pathogen available to infect the host plant

entities.name: GROWTH STAGES
entities.description: Developmental phases of the chickpea plant including seedling, post-seedling, vegetative, flowering, and podding

entities.name: RESISTANCE BREEDING PROGRAM
entities.description: A systematic approach to develop chickpea varieties with genetic resistance to Ascochyta blight

entities.name: SPRAY INOCULATION
entities.description: The process of applying a conidial suspension of A. rabiei to plants to induce infection

entities.name: CONTROLLED ENVIRONMENT
entities.description: Regulated environmental conditions used to conduct inoculation experiments

entities.name: INCUBATION PERIOD
entities.description: The time interval between the inoculation of the pathogen and the appearance of symptoms

entities.name: DISEASE SEVERITY
entities.description: The measurement of the extent of Ascochyta blight infection on the plant

entities.name: ADULT PLANT FIELD SCREENING
entities.description: The evaluation of disease resistance in mature plants within field settings

entities.name: DHAULAKUAN
entities.description: A specific hot-spot location in India used for field screening

entities.name: LUDHIANA
entities.description: A specific hot-spot location in India used for field screening

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: The country where the field screening locations are situated

entities.name: ICRISAT
entities.description: The research institution where controlled environment evaluations were performed
